en_body:
from the tree of birth-and-death branches flower
thrusting across, growing backward:
 becoming a dream!
...mù-u sweet blossom, blossom drenched in bloom...
blazing the season of anguish, the thorn-body
close to blanket, pillow, earth plowed into furrows of
 tomorrow
...rising into the afterward
 a tree of Human-Love.

...............................................................
(a poem for my Christ Jesus)
...............................................................

Tilling the Earth of Human-Life

from the tree of birth-and-death branches flower
thrusting across, growing backward:
 becoming a dream!
...mù-u sweet blossom, blossom drenched in bloom...
blazing the season of anguish, the thorn-body
close to blanket, pillow, earth plowed into furrows of
 tomorrow
...rising into the afterward
 a tree of Human-Love.

...............................................................
(a poem for my Christ Jesus)
...............................................................

Tilling the Earth of Human-Life

from the tree of birth-and-death branches flower
thrusting across, growing backward:
 becoming a dream!
...mù-u sweet blossom, blossom drenched in bloom...
blazing the season of anguish, the thorn-body
close to blanket, pillow, earth plowed into furrows of
 tomorrow
...rising into the afterward
 a tree of Human-Love.

...............................................................
(a poem for my Christ Jesus)
...............................................................
note: The “tree of birth-and-death” invokes Buddhist samsara, while the thorn-body and dedication to Christ braid that image with the Crucifixion.
